message:
feat(resolv): port libresolv DNS message parser — ns_initparse + ns_parserr + ns_skiprr + ns_msg_getflag (bd-kf793)

Add the libresolv DNS message parsing API. These four functions plus
the ns_msg / ns_rr structs let callers walk a wire-format DNS message
and pull out individual resource records.

  - CNsMsg / CNsRr: #[repr(C)] structs matching glibc's
    <arpa/nameser.h> layout exactly (80 / 1048 bytes on x86_64) so a
    handle written by ns_initparse here can be inspected by other
    libresolv-shaped code.
  - ns_initparse(msg, msglen, *handle): parse the 12-byte header
    (id / flags / 4 section counts), then walk each section
    (qd/an/ns/ar) to find the per-section start pointer. Returns -1
    on any overrun or malformed name.
  - ns_skiprr(ptr, eom, section, count): skip `count` records and
    return the bytes consumed. Section 0 entries are name + 4 bytes;
    sections 1-3 are name + 10 fixed + rdlength.
  - ns_parserr(handle, section, rrnum, *rr): walk to the n-th record
    in the given section, expand its name via dn_expand, and fill
    the CNsRr with type/class/ttl/rdlength/rdata. The rdata pointer
    points back into the original message buffer (no copy).
  - ns_msg_getflag(handle, flag): extract a header bit per the
    ns_flag enum (QR / opcode / AA / TC / RD / RA / Z / AD / CD /
    RCODE) per RFC 1035 layout.

Built atop our existing dn_skipname + dn_expand helpers in
unistd_abi.rs.

Tests: 9 new abi tests using a synthetic DNS response message
("foo.com" A IN 127.0.0.1 with the answer using a compression pointer
back to the question). Covers happy-path parse + flags + rdata
roundtrip, rejection of truncated headers and truncated sections,
out-of-range rrnum and section index, and skiprr advance + overrun
guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
